heading: From founding practice to public-company platform.

events.title: Family hearing-care practice founded
events.summary: Marvin Posey opens a hearing-aid practice in Central California, the first chapter of what becomes a multi-generation hearing-care life.

events.title: Next generation steps in
events.summary: Mark and Kim Moore are running the family clinic; a young Matthew Moore (today INND's CEO) grows up inside the fitting rooms and workbenches.

events.title: InnerScope enters the public markets
events.summary: Family-rooted operating experience meets capital-markets infrastructure. Listed on OTC as INND.

events.title: Step-function growth via acquisitions
events.summary: Acquires iHEAR Medical (FDA-cleared technology + teleaudiology platform) and Hearing Assist II LLC (Walmart's largest dedicated hearing-aid supplier at the time).

events.title: First profitable full year
events.summary: $15.28M net revenue, $5.58M net profit, ~36% earnings-before-tax margin — unusually profitable for a microcap hearing-tech operator.

events.title: iHEAR Matrix lands in 4,218 Walmart stores
events.summary: Wholesale vendor selection places iHEAR Matrix Rechargeable OTC Hearing Aids in Walmart's nationwide OTC Hearing Aid Display.

events.title: OTCHealthMart.com launches
events.summary: Direct-to-consumer e-commerce platform for OTC hearing aids alongside vitamins, supplements, and (planned) durable medical equipment for the 55+ demographic.

events.title: Strategic alliance with Ainnova / OTCHealth
events.summary: InnerScope joins the announced acquisition of OTCHealth Inc. by Ainnova Tech Inc. — receiving equity and profit-participation rights in both entities while refocusing on proprietary hearing-technology R&D.
